Repro: with one bead closed on 2026-01-01, one correlated session on 2026-01-01, and only `updated_at=2026-03-05`, `Summary(Query{Since: 2026-02-01, HasSince: true})` returns `beads.total=1 closed=1 unknown_cost=1 cost.beads=1` while sessions, cycle-time, and rework are all zero. SD-016 says `--since` means include only facts observed on or after the cutoff for the relevant fact type; a tracker metadata edit after the window is not a new lifecycle or cost fact.\nSuggested fix: Define summary bead inclusion from the same fact timelines used by the underlying sections, rather than raw `updated_at`. A bead with no in-window close/reopen/session facts should not surface in the windowed summary or its cost/feature counts, and regression coverage should include the late-update/no-facts case.","acceptance":"Given a bead whose first close and only correlated sessions are before the cutoff but whose updated_at is later, metrics summary --since excludes it from bead totals and cost/feature unknown counts; a regression test covers the late-update/no-facts case; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/processmetrics -count=1 passes","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:api"],"bead":{"id":"hx-17a99906","title":"correctness: summary since window must ignore late-updated pre-cutoff beads","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:285\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new summary window helper uses bead `updated_at` as the inclusion gate, so a bead whose close fact and only correlated sessions are both before the cutoff still appears in `ddx metrics summary --since ...` if it was edited later for unrelated reasons. `beadsVisibleInSummaryWindow` admits that bead, and the downstream summary logic counts it in `summary.Beads.Total/Closed`, `summary.Beads.UnknownCost`, `summary.Cost.Beads`, and `summary.Cost.Features` even though there are no in-window lifecycle or session facts. Outcome is populated post-execution from the existing final-event status fields.\n\nWhen a pin fails pre-dispatch (orphan route-target, unknown upstream-service, runner/upstream-service incompatibility), emit a 'rejected_override' event with the same shape minus outcome.\n\nprompt_features.estimated_tokens uses ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens if provided, else best-effort runner-level tokenizer (nullable).\n\nreason_hint accepts a free-form string from Metadata[\"override.reason\"] (programmatic) or a future --override-reason CLI flag.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- service.go (event payload struct definitions)\n- service_events.go (event type constants + decoded event mapping)\n- service_execute.go (override capture + event emission, second ResolveRoute call)\n- service_execute_test.go (event-shape and emission-order tests)\n\nOut of scope:\n- RouteStatus / UsageReport metric exposure (bead 2)\n- CLI surface (bead 3)\n- Any change to existing routing-decision events\n- Automatic learning loop","acceptance":"1. ExecuteRequest with Runner, Upstream-service, or Route-target set produces exactly one override event before the final event. Verified in TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal across all three axes individually and in combination.\n3. The override event's auto_decision field is computed from a second ResolveRoute call with the override axes stripped. When pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires (coincidental-agreement case). Verified in TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement.\n4. axes_overridden lists exactly the axes the caller pinned (subset of {runner, upstream-service, route-target}). Verified in TestRejectedOverrideEventOnOrphanRoute-target.\n7. prompt_features.estimated_tokens reflects ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens when set; nullable otherwise. for execute-bead evidence capture | ./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:490:**Decisive C-favoring argument: comparison suite.** A stated project goal is that ddx-agent's native harness should *outperform* the subprocess wrappers (claude/codex/opencode) on batch noninteractive tasks. Proving that requires a comparison suite that runs identical prompts through every harness implementation and measures latency, cost, success rate, output quality. Today that suite is split: harness invocation lives in DDx (`cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `cli/internal/agent/benchmark.go`), routing lives in DDx, but the native agent runtime lives in ddx-agent. Under C, the comparison suite collapses into one module that can A/B test (`harness=claude` subprocess) vs (`harness=agent-native`, `provider=bragi`, `model=qwen3.6`) with the same input/output schema and metrics collection. Under A or B, the suite still has to bridge two modules and measurement parity is harder to maintain. ./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:938:| `routing_metrics_test.go` | 84 | Routing metric aggregation | ./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:997:- 3.5.4 **Report.** Tabular comparison: candidate × test → metrics. ./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:59:| D5 | **Wire transport**: GraphQL only. | All DDx↔Axon traffic is GraphQL — queries and mutations over HTTP, subscriptions over WebSocket. No gRPC, no REST, no MCP from the DDx side. The Go client is generated by `genqlient` from a pinned schema-introspection snapshot. Rationale: Axon's GraphQL surface already covers entity CRUD, links, traversal, transactions, schema, and subscriptions; DDx is already a GraphQL server internally (`cli/internal/server/graphql_*.go`), so DDx↔Axon is wire-symmetric; subscriptions unlock live UI updates for `ddx work` (Story 8) without polling; no protoc/codegen toolchain enters the DDx build. \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! Adds tables for:\\n4\\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\\n5\\t//! - users: global user identities\\n6\\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\\n7\\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\\n8\\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\\n9\\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\\n10\\t//!\\n11\\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\\n12\\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\\n13\\t\\n14\\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t updated_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t )\\\",\\n29\\t )\\n30\\t .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t // Create users table.\\n33\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t email TEXT,\\n38\\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t suspended_at_ms INTEGER\\n40\\t )\\\",\\n41\\t )\\n42\\t .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t external_id TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t )\\\",\\n54\\t )\\n55\\t .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t role TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t added_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t )\\\",\\n67\\t )\\n68\\t .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t database_name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t created_at_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, database_name)\\n77\\t )\\\",\\n78\\t )\\n79\\t .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_databases table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n80\\t\\n81\\t // Create credential_revocations table.\\n82\\t conn.execute_batch(\\n83\\t \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS credential_revocations (\\n84\\t jti TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n85\\t Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension. Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`. If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//! └─ authenticate_http_request (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//! ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer → Identity\\n36\\t//! └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//! └─ route handler\\n38\\t//! ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//! ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//! └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n }\\n\\n /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n encode(\\n \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n claims,\\n \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n )\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n }\\n\\n /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n ///\\n /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n validation.validate_exp = false;\\n validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n validation.validate_aud = false;\\n validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n .map(|td| td.claims)\\n .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n })\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n /// Create an empty cache.\\n pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n Self::default()\\n }\\n\\n /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n self.inner\\n .read()\\n .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n .unwrap_or(false)\\n }\\n\\n /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n g.insert(jti);\\n }\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n /// Convenience constructor.\\n pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n Self {\\n issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n }\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n/// .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n/// .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n/// Arc::new(state),\\n/// jwt_verify_layer,\\n/// ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n request: axum::extract::Request,\\n next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n return next.run(request).await;\\n };\\n\\n let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n let database = database.to_string();\\n let method = request.method().clone();\\n let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n let mut request = request;\\n request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n next.run(request).await\\n }\\n Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n method: \u0026Method,\\n tenant: \u0026str,\\n database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n // Step 3: Check iss.\\n if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n let now = SystemTime::now()\\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n .unwrap_or_default()\\n .as_secs();\\n const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now ↔ expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n }\\n // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew ↔ valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n .jti\\n .parse()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n }\\n {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n if storage\\n .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n {\\n // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n drop(storage);\\n state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n }\\n }\\n\\n // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n if claims.aud != tenant {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n let user = {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n storage\\n .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n };\\n if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n storage\\n .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n }\\n\\n // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n let granted_db = claims\\n .grants\\n .find_database(database)\\n .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n Op::Read\\n } else {\\n Op::Write\\n };\\n if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n }\\n\\n Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n user_id,\\n tenant_id,\\n grants: claims.grants,\\n })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n let auth_header = headers\\n .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n let auth_str = auth_header\\n .to_str()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n auth_str\\n .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n {\\n let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n if database.is_empty() {\\n None\\n } else {\\n Some((tenant, database))\\n }\\n }\\n _ =\u003e None,\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n let body = json!({\\n \\\"error\\\": {\\n \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n }\\n });\\n (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123 # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	ddxexec "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/exec"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/metric"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newMetricTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func writeMetricArtifact(t *testing.T, workingDir, id, title string) {
	t.Helper()
	artifactPath := filepath.Join(workingDir, "docs", "metrics", id+".md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(artifactPath), 0o755))
	if title == "" {
		title = id
	}
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(artifactPath, []byte("---\nddx:\n  id: "+id+"\n---\n# "+title+"\n"), 0o644))
}

func writeMetricFixture(t *testing.T, workingDir string) {
	t.Helper()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, workingDir, "MET-001", "Metric Startup Time")

	store := ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.SaveDefinition(ddxexec.Definition{
		ID:          "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline",
		ArtifactIDs: []string{"MET-001"},
		Executor: ddxexec.ExecutorSpec{
			Kind:    ddxexec.ExecutorKindCommand,
			Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '20ms\\n'"},
		},
		Result: ddxexec.ResultSpec{Metric: &ddxexec.MetricResultSpec{Unit: "ms"}},
		Evaluation: ddxexec.Evaluation{
			Comparison: metric.ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
			Thresholds: ddxexec.Thresholds{WarnMS: 20, RatchetMS: 30},
		},
		Active:    true,
		CreatedAt: mustMetricTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	}))
	require.NoError(t, store.SaveDefinition(ddxexec.Definition{
		ID:          "exec-metric-startup-time@current",
		ArtifactIDs: []string{"MET-001"},
		Executor: ddxexec.ExecutorSpec{
			Kind:    ddxexec.ExecutorKindCommand,
			Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '14.6ms\\n'"},
		},
		Result: ddxexec.ResultSpec{Metric: &ddxexec.MetricResultSpec{Unit: "ms"}},
		Evaluation: ddxexec.Evaluation{
			Comparison: metric.ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
			Thresholds: ddxexec.Thresholds{WarnMS: 20, RatchetMS: 30},
		},
		Active:    true,
		CreatedAt: mustMetricTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z"),
	}))
}

func TestMetricCommandsValidateRunHistoryAndCompare(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricFixture(t, workingDir)

	factory := newMetricTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "list", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var artifacts []struct {
		ID    string `json:"id"`
		Title string `json:"title"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &artifacts))
	require.Len(t, artifacts, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", artifacts[0].ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "Metric Startup Time", artifacts[0].Title)

	validateOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "validate", "MET-001")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, validateOut, "validated")

	execRunOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "exec", "run", "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var execRun ddxexec.RunRecord
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(execRunOut), &execRun))
	assert.Equal(t, "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline", execRun.DefinitionID)

	runOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "run", "MET-001", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var run metric.HistoryRecord
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(runOut), &run))
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", run.MetricID)
	assert.Equal(t, metric.StatusPass, run.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "exec-metric-startup-time@current", run.DefinitionID)
	assert.InDelta(t, 14.6, run.Value, 0.01)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "show", "MET-001", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var show struct {
		Artifact struct {
			ID    string `json:"id"`
			Title string `json:"title"`
		} `json:"artifact"`
		Definition struct {
			DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
		} `json:"definition"`
		RecentHistory []metric.HistoryRecord `json:"recent_history"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &show))
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", show.Artifact.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "exec-metric-startup-time@current", show.Definition.DefinitionID)
	require.Len(t, show.RecentHistory, 2)
	assert.Equal(t, "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline", show.RecentHistory[0].DefinitionID)
	assert.Equal(t, "exec-metric-startup-time@current", show.RecentHistory[1].DefinitionID)

	historyOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "history", "MET-001", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var history []metric.HistoryGroup
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(historyOut), &history))
	require.Len(t, history, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "ms", history[0].Unit)
	require.Len(t, history[0].Records, 2)

	compareOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "compare", "MET-001", "--against", "baseline", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, compareOut, "comparison")
	assert.Contains(t, strings.ToLower(compareOut), "baseline")

	trendOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "trend", "MET-001", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var trend metric.TrendSummary
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(trendOut), &trend))
	assert.Equal(t, 2, trend.Count)
	assert.Equal(t, "MET-001", trend.MetricID)
}

func TestMetricCommandsRefuseMixedUnitsAndGroupHistoryByUnit(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	writeMetricArtifact(t, workingDir, "MET-001", "")

	store := ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.SaveDefinition(ddxexec.Definition{
		ID:          "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline",
		ArtifactIDs: []string{"MET-001"},
		Executor: ddxexec.ExecutorSpec{
			Kind:    ddxexec.ExecutorKindCommand,
			Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '20ms\\n'"},
		},
		Result: ddxexec.ResultSpec{Metric: &ddxexec.MetricResultSpec{Unit: "ms"}},
		Evaluation: ddxexec.Evaluation{
			Comparison: metric.ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
			Thresholds: ddxexec.Thresholds{WarnMS: 20, RatchetMS: 30},
		},
		Active:    true,
		CreatedAt: mustMetricTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"),
	}))
	require.NoError(t, store.SaveDefinition(ddxexec.Definition{
		ID:          "exec-metric-startup-time@usd",
		ArtifactIDs: []string{"MET-001"},
		Executor: ddxexec.ExecutorSpec{
			Kind:    ddxexec.ExecutorKindCommand,
			Command: []string{"sh", "-c", "printf '10USD\\n'"},
		},
		Result: ddxexec.ResultSpec{Metric: &ddxexec.MetricResultSpec{Unit: "USD"}},
		Evaluation: ddxexec.Evaluation{
			Comparison: metric.ComparisonLowerIsBetter,
			Thresholds: ddxexec.Thresholds{WarnMS: 20, RatchetMS: 30},
		},
		Active:    true,
		CreatedAt: mustMetricTime(t, "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z"),
	}))

	factory := newMetricTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "exec", "run", "exec-metric-startup-time@baseline")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "exec", "run", "exec-metric-startup-time@usd")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "compare", "MET-001", "--against", "baseline")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, strings.ToLower(err.Error()), "mixed units")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "trend", "MET-001")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, strings.ToLower(err.Error()), "mixed units")

	historyOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "metric", "history", "MET-001", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var grouped []metric.HistoryGroup
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(historyOut), &grouped))
	require.Len(t, grouped, 2)
	assert.Equal(t, "ms", grouped[0].Unit)
	assert.Equal(t, "USD", grouped[1].Unit)
	require.Len(t, grouped[0].Records, 1)
	require.Len(t, grouped[1].Records, 1)
}

func mustMetricTime(t *testing.T, value string) time.Time {
	t.Helper()
	parsed, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, value)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	return parsed
}
